title: Pakistan Birth Certificate Guide – Union Council & NADRA Wizard (2026 Updated)
legal_basis: Births and Deaths Registration Act + NADRA Ordinance 2000 + Provincial CRVS Laws (2026). First step always: Union Council Computerized Birth Registration Certificate (CBRC). Punjab: Free registration & computerized certificate up to 7 years late (CM policy 2026). NADRA CRC fees: Normal Rs 50, Urgent/Executive Rs 500.

wizard.title: Birth Registration Wizard
wizard.subtitle: Union Council CBRC & NADRA B-Form • 2026 Verified

whats_this.heading: Birth Certificate Wizard
whats_this.description: A Union Council birth certificate is an official civil document issued by local Union Councils, Municipal Corporations, Cantonment Boards, or the Capital Development Authority (CDA) in Pakistan. It serves as primary evidence of birth and is required for US immigration processes (USCIS, NVC, and Embassy interviews).
quick_instructions: Answer a few questions about who needs the certificate
Punjab residents enjoy free registration for up to 7 years late (no court order needed).
Biometric requirements vary by age: 0-3 years (None), 3-10 years (Photo), 10-18 years (Full fingerprints/Iris).
whats_this.disclaimer: This wizard provides general guidance based on publicly available information and should not be considered legal advice. Requirements may vary by Union Council and can change over time. Always verify current procedures with your local office and consult an immigration attorney for specific legal guidance.

document_need.title: What do you require regarding birth certificates?
document_need.description: Select the option that best describes your current need.

types.title: New Registration
types.description: First-time CBRC registration at Union Council or NADRA B-Form issuance.

types.title: Correction
types.description: Fixing name, DOB, or parent spelling errors in existing records.

types.title: Replacement / Duplicate
types.description: Reissuing certificates that were lost or damaged.

age_category.title: Select the person's current age:
age_category.description: Age group determines biometric requirements and necessary proofs.

options.title: 0 to 3 Years
options.description: Infant. No photo, no biometrics required at NADRA (only parents present).

options.title: 3 to 10 Years
options.description: Child. Photo mandatory; iris optional at advanced centers.

options.title: 10 to 18 Years
options.description: Minor. Fingerprints, photo, and iris all mandatory.

options.title: 18 Years and Above
options.description: Adult. Full biometrics for CNIC. Family biometrics option available if CBRC missing (till Dec 31, 2026).

birth_setting.title: Where did the birth occur?
birth_setting.description: Evidence requirements change based on birth setting.

types.title: In a Hospital
types.description: Requires discharge slip or birth notification slip.

types.title: Abroad / Overseas
types.description: Requires Embassy S-1 form or consulate notification.

stations.title: Gather Evidence
stations.description: Collect mandatory proofs for your specific age group and setting.

stations.title: Union Council (CBRC)
stations.description: Step 1: Obtain the Computerized Birth Registration Certificate.

stations.title: NADRA B-Form
stations.description: Step 2: Obtain the CRC/B-Form or Adult CNIC.
tasks: Pay fee: Normal Rs 50 / Urgent Rs 500

stations.title: Final Collection
stations.description: Collect the final computerized B-Form or CNIC.
tasks: Track status via SMS or 1777 Helpline

very_late: Others: Court decree or Newspaper Ad
18_plus: First-time CNIC: Family biometrics option if CBRC missing (Till Dec 2026)

tips: Union Council CBRC is mandatory before NADRA B-Form application.
Punjab: Registration is free up to 7 years late (CM policy 2026).
pitfalls: Applying at NADRA center without a Computerized UC Certificate (CBRC).

tips: 18+: Use family biometrics verification if birth cert is missing (Till Dec 31, 2026).
0-3 Years: No biometrics or photo required at center.
pitfalls: Expired parent CNICs are not accepted for registration.

tips: Home births require an affidavit signed by parents and 2 witnesses.
Vaccination card is strong evidence for home births.
pitfalls: Hospital slips are required to avoid 'Late' classification in hospital births.

tips: Pak-ID app allows full online UC registration in Punjab.
NADRA Helpline (1777) is available for all provinces.
pitfalls: Applying in the wrong UC jurisdiction will lead to rejection.

tips: Both parents' CNICs are preferred for smooth B-Form issuance.
Married couples should ensure names match Nikah Nama exactly.
pitfalls: Spelling mismatches cause major delays in immigration RFEs.

tips: Urgent/Executive B-Form (Rs 500) is usually ready in 3-5 days.
Normal B-Form fee is only Rs 50.
pitfalls: Spelling errors in initial UC registration are hard to fix later.
